      "それははしごと橋のような概念を持っています。",
      "それはクー・テュルクを持っています。",
      "それは斜め打ちを持っています。",
      "それはキングズ・トラップを持っています。",
      "それは強制キャプチャーを持っています。"

      "それはダブルグリッドと駒の線形移動によって特徴づけられます。",

      "それはブリッジによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはクー・テュルクによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは斜め打ちによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それはキングズ・トラップによって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは線形移動によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは反撃のために2つ以上の駒の犠牲によって特徴づけられます。",
      "それは取られた駒がターンの終わりに除去されることによって特徴づけられます。"
    ],
    "UsedFor": [
      "それは物質的優位性のための盤面をまたぐ複数のキャプチャーに使用されます。",
      "それはキングへの昇格につながる位置的優位性のために使用されます。",
      "それはラダーストライクのセットアップに使用されます。",
      "それは相手の駒またはキングを有利な位置に操作するために使用されます。",
      "それはマジョリティキャプチャーによる反撃に使用されます。",
      "それは線形移動を利用した防御に使用されます。",
      "それは競技プレイに使用されます。",
      "それはオンライン大会に使用されます。"
    ],

IsA: It is an abstract strategy board game.
It is a variant of the game draughts.
It is a variant of checkers.
HasA: It has 18 pieces per player.
It has a double grid.
It has linear movement of men.
It has a distinctive trapezoid shape for piece arrangement.
It has a fundamental part called majority capture.
It has concepts such as ladders and bridges.
It has the coup turc.
It has the oblique hit.
It has the king's trap.
It has forced capture.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its double grid and linear movement of men.
It is characterized by majority capture.
It is characterized by ladder strikes.
It is characterized by bridges.
It is characterized by the coup turc.
It is characterized by the oblique hit.
It is characterized by the king's trap.
It is characterized by linear movement.
It is characterized by the sacrifice of two or more men for counterattack.
It is characterized by the removal of captured men at the end of the turn.
UsedFor: It is used for board spanning multiple captures for material advantage.
It is used for positional advantage ending in promotion to a king.
It is used for setting up ladder strikes.
It is used for manipulating an opponent's man or king to an advantageous position.
It is used for counterattacks with a majority capture.
It is used for defense that takes advantage of linear movement.
It is used for competitive play.
It is used for online tournaments.
LocatedIn: It is located on an 8x8 checkered gameboard.
OriginatedIn: It originated in Christian Freeling's 1988 game Bushka.
It originated in the traditional Madagascar game Fanorona.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by Christian Freeling.
InventedBy: It was invented by Christian Freeling.
PublishedIn: It was published in Abstract Games magazine.
Affects: It affects the speed of the game.
It affects the percentage of draws.
ConsistsOf: It consists of 18 pieces per player.
It consists of rules regarding linear movement and maximum capture.
PartOf: It is part of the abstract strategy board game genre.
It is part of the draughts/checkers family of games.
KnownFor: It is known for its unique tactics and strategies.
It is known for majority capture.
It is known for the coup turc.
It is known for the oblique hit.
It is known for the king's trap.
It is known for having very few draws.
It is known for its game tree complexity.
It is known for being an attempt to perfect checkers.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Christian Freeling.
It is associated with the game draughts.
It is associated with the game checkers.
It is associated with the game Bushka.
It is associated with the game Fanorona.
It is associated with the game Croda.
It is associated with abstract game designers.
It is associated with online tournament platforms.
It is associated with strategy books.
About: It is about strategy and tactics in a board game.
It is about the mechanics of capture in board games.
It is about the evolution of abstract strategy games.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the game draughts.
It was influenced by the game checkers.
It was influenced by the game Bushka.
It was influenced by the game Fanorona.
It was influenced by the game Croda.
It was influenced by Christian Freeling's ideas on game mechanics.
It was influenced by Ljuban Dedić's work on checkers variants.
Influenced: It influenced the development of strategy books.
It influenced discussions on abstract strategy games.
